project_information.project_title.answer: STEM Makerspace: Community Problem-Solving Through Design Thinking

project_information.subjects.answer: Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics, Art (STEAM), Computer Science

project_information.students_target_grou.answer: Primary target group consists of approximately 90 7th-grade students enrolled in core science classes, including a mix of general education, English language learners, and students with IEPs who are often underrepresented in advanced STEM opportunities. These students are part of a diverse, Title I school community, with many coming from low-income households and having limited access to technology and hands-on STEM enrichment outside of school.

project_information.description_of_the_p.answer: This project will establish a mobile STEM makerspace that travels between 6th–8th grade classrooms, enabling students to use design thinking and hands-on engineering to solve real community problems. Students will work in teams to identify issues (such as school recycling, accessibility, or neighborhood safety) and prototype solutions using low-cost materials, microcontrollers, and 3D-printed components. The grant will fund portable carts, basic electronics kits, and consumable supplies. Classroom teachers will receive training and co-teach project-based units, culminating in a school-wide “Community Solutions Expo” where students present their designs to peers, staff, and local partners.
